title: Codex 2026 Prompting and Repository Practice · Prompt Forensics
summary: Apply current Codex guidance across CLI, IDE, app, planning, and repository instructions. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.
Use Plan mode or an interview when requirements are fuzzy or choices are consequential.
Place recurring build, test, lint, review, and boundary guidance in the nearest applicable AGENTS.md.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Codex Four Parts trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.

content.text: For Codex Four Parts, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Codex Four Parts precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Codex requires a rigid magic prompt syntax.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-codex-review-verify-1ku727" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Codex Review Verify into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-codex-review-verify-1ku727
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Codex Review Verify precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Codex Review Verify applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prompt Codex to test, inspect the diff, review risk, and report evidence before completion.
